summary: Frontend-only testing of Yuval Cohen portfolio (https://yuval-journey.preview.emergentagent.com). All critical flows verified: hero loads cleanly with rotating roles + CTAs, Download CV link reaches /Yuval-Cohen-CV.pdf (HTTP 200), Let's Talk opens the contact modal on desktop & mobile (form inputs, submit, copy-email, close all work), modal closes, Journey panel and all 4 nav items present on desktop and trigger smooth scroll to corresponding sections (about/experience/tools/works), the 4 ride checkpoint cards render in DOM, all detailed sections (about / experience / tools / works / connect) render with full content (5 capability cards, 4 experience stations, 9 work cards, animated stat count-ups, campfire CTA + connect-lets-talk + email link). Mobile (390x844) hides Journey panel and shows the slim top progress bar; no horizontal overflow; modal usable. NO uncaught runtime errors, NO console errors, NO WAAPI 'monotonically non-decreasing' offset error.

issues: Rotating role text 'AI & 3D SPECIALIST' visually extends past the 390px viewport edge during its rotation (the H1 is sized text-4xl on mobile). Document scrollWidth still equals viewport (no horizontal scrollbar) because the parent clips it, but visually the right side of the longest role string is cut off slightly. Consider reducing the mobile font size for the rotating heading or letting the longest role wrap to two lines.

action_items: Optional polish: slightly reduce mobile font-size of the rotating role H1 so 'AI & 3D SPECIALIST' fits cleanly within 390px (currently text-4xl => ~36px). e.g., add a responsive clamp or change to text-[2rem] on small screens.
critical_code_review_comments: ContactModal submit is a <button type='submit'> that sets window.location.href = mailto:... inside onSubmit. It does not expose an href attribute — that is correct behavior. Any future test that checks 'href' on contact-submit will get null; verify via form submission instead.
Hero's Let's Talk button uses window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('open-contact')) — clean, decoupled. Modal hooks via useEffect listener. Works on desktop and mobile.
Lenis smooth scroll: handleJump('anchor') works; journey-nav clicks scrolled to correct sections (verified ABOUT and WORKS).
No backend exists (frontend-only build) — confirmed; no /api routes referenced.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only portfolio site. No backend. All data-testid attributes from the spec are present and functional. Use page.click without force=True for modal triggers — observed intermittent behavior with force=True on mobile during the Lenis-driven layout. Lenis blocks window.scrollTo; use page.mouse.wheel or click journey-nav items to navigate. Hero rotating role overflows slightly on mobile widths — minor polish item only.
rca of the issue: No critical bugs found. Minor design observation: rotating role text can visually overflow on smallest mobile widths due to text-4xl size + longest role string ('AI & 3D SPECIALIST'). Mitigation: scale font down on mobile or allow line wrap.
